sourceMovie.title: Waves
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Waves works as a romance movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The romance and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Remember Me
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Waves clicked for you, Remember Me (2010) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Waves?
faq.answer: Try Remember Me, The Virgin Suicides, West Side Story—each shares DNA with Waves while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Waves?
faq.answer: Waves (2019) is best described as Romance, Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Waves worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
